note_data.title: Basic Statistics
keyPoints: Statistics is the science which deals with the collection, presentation, analysis and interpretation of numerical data
Data can be classified as Primary Data (collected directly) and Secondary Data (obtained from other sources)
Measures of Central Tendency include Mean, Median and Mode - these show the central value of data
Arithmetic Mean = Sum of all observations ÷ Number of observations
For grouped data, Mean = Σfx/Σf where f is frequency and x is class mark
Median is the middle value when data is arranged in ascending or descending order
Mode is the value that occurs most frequently in the data set
Range = Highest value - Lowest value, measures the spread of data
Frequency Distribution Table organizes raw data into classes with their frequencies

definitions.definition: Statistics is the science which deals with the collection, presentation, analysis and interpretation of numerical data.
Primary data is the data collected directly by the investigator from the original source for the first time.
The arithmetic mean of a set of observations is the sum of all observations divided by the number of observations.
The median is the middle value of the observations when they are arranged in ascending or descending order of magnitude.
The mode is the value which occurs most frequently in a set of observations.

importantQuestions.question: Define Primary Data and Secondary Data with examples.
importantQuestions.answer: Primary Data: Data collected directly by investigator from original source. Example: Survey of students' heights in your class. Secondary Data: Data obtained from other sources. Example: Population data from census reports.

importantQuestions.question: Find the mean, median and mode of the following data: Marks of 30 students in Mathematics test in a school in Karachi: 45, 50, 55, 45, 60, 50, 45, 65, 70, 50, 55, 45, 60, 55, 50, 65, 45, 55, 60, 50, 70, 55, 45, 60, 50, 55, 65, 45, 60, 55
importantQuestions.answer: Mean = Sum of all marks ÷ 30 = 1665 ÷ 30 = 55.5 marks. For Median: Arrange in order, middle values are 15th and 16th = (55+55) ÷ 2 = 55 marks. Mode: Most frequent value is 45 and 50 and 55 (each appears 7 times) - trimodal distribution.

mcqs.question: If the mean of 5 numbers is 12, what is the sum of these numbers?
The median of the data 3, 7, 5, 9, 11, 2, 8 is:
Data collected from census reports is an example of:

commonMistakes: Students often forget to arrange data in order before finding median
Confusing class mark with class interval in grouped data mean calculation
Not writing the formula before solving numerical problems
Mixing up definitions of primary and secondary data
Forgetting to divide by total frequency (Σf) in grouped data mean formula
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question about definitions in every BSEK paper
Long question on calculating mean, median, mode appears every year
Always write complete definitions word-for-word from textbook
Show all steps clearly in numerical problems for full marks
Practice frequency distribution table construction with Pakistani context data
formulas: Median position = (n+1)/2 for odd n, average of n/2 and (n/2+1) positions for even n
note_data.keyDiagram: Frequency Distribution Table showing Class Intervals, Class Marks, Frequency columns with proper labeling and totals. Also practice drawing simple bar graphs and histograms for data representation.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define statistics and distinguish between primary and secondary data with examples
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required calculation of mean, median and mode from given frequency distribution table
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (3 marks) — asked to define population, sample and variable with one example each
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q8 (5 marks) — asked students to construct frequency table and calculate arithmetic mean from ungrouped data

workedExample.question: Calculate the arithmetic mean of the following data: 15, 18, 12, 20, 16, 14, 19, 13, 17, 11 (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Add all the given values: 15 + 18 + 12 + 20 + 16 + 14 + 19 + 13 + 17 + 11 = 155
Step 2: Count the total number of observations: n = 10
Step 3: Apply the formula for arithmetic mean: Mean = Sum of all observations ÷ Number of observations
workedExample.answer: The arithmetic mean of the given data is 15.5
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct sum (155), 1 mark for identifying n=10, 1 mark for correct formula application, 1 mark for final answer

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for definitions of statistics, population, and sample.
Common error: Writing 'average' instead of 'arithmetic mean' in calculations. BSEK expects precise mathematical terminology as given in the textbook.
Many students forget to show step-by-step working in statistical calculations. Always write the formula first, then substitute values, and show complete working for full marks.
note_data.quickRecap: Statistics is the science of collecting, organizing, presenting, analyzing and interpreting numerical data. Remember the exact textbook definitions of population (complete group), sample (part of population), variable (characteristic that changes), primary data (collected directly) and secondary data (already collected). For calculations, always show complete working with formulas for mean, median, and mode.
importantFormulas: Arithmetic Mean = Sum of all observations ÷ Number of observations
For median: arrange data in ascending order, if n is odd: median = (n+1)/2 th term, if n is even: median = average of n/2 and (n/2 + 1) terms
